content: Vd is calculated from plasma concentration measurements after IV administration: Vd = Dose / C0 (concentration at time zero, extrapolated from elimination curve). Apparent Vd at steady state (Vdss) accounts for tissue equilibration. Central volume (Vc) represents initial distribution space before tissue equilibration. Multi-compartment models describe drugs with distinct distribution and elimination phases. Factors affecting Vd: molecular size (large molecules stay in plasma), lipophilicity (lipid-soluble drugs have high Vd), protein binding (high binding reduces Vd), tissue binding (increases Vd), ionization (charged molecules have lower Vd). Pathologic states alter Vd: burns increase Vd due to capillary leak; dehydration decreases Vd.
